description: CAPT-SPEC 2026-07-12 (v4.0.0) — partMass_g now comes from a DRAWING THAT EXISTS. Until today this recipe demanded SOLL 10.35 g with toleranceSource 'drawing', and there was no drawing: erp.articles.gewicht_kg was NULL for all 222 articles, qms.characteristics carried no injection-moulding characteristic at all, and the recipe itself admitted the bands 'mirror the housing-A SOLL' — an article that does not exist in the 222-article master. Meanwhile machine.ts drew part masses as normal(partMassTargetG, 0.8 %), zero-mean noise about the machine's own config constant, so the machine's true mean was exactly 10.40 g. The celebrated Ca of 0.147 was thus the distance between a sim constant and a typed number, wearing a provenance that pointed at nothing. --- WHAT IS TRUE NOW. The part sgm-004 actually runs is _V4_12-0044-003 ("Steckergehäuse Typ C"), and it already had a RELEASED drawing in the PLM (DWG-12-0044-003) whose content column was NULL. The place existed; it was empty. It now holds the geometry, and the band below is DERIVED from it: cavity volume 7.936 cm3 x 1.36 g/cm3 (PA66-GF30, ISO 1183) = 10.79 g nominal, and the limits [10.31, 11.27] are NOT a second typed number — they are the drawing's wall-thickness tolerance (2 +/- 0.1 mm) propagated through the same volume formula. Because the number now comes from the product's geometry, toleranceSource 'drawing' finally RESOLVES: windchill.documents -> DWG-12-0044-003. The band is BINDING and may not be widened to make a deviation vanish — widening it would mean re-drawing the part. --- THE MACHINE STILL DEVIATES, AND NOW FOR A REASON. The sim no longer carries a magic mass offset. It moulds the cavity (7.936 cm3) and the melt is PACKED: part mass rises with holding pressure, which is exactly why a setter weighs parts to dial Nachdruck in. sgm-004's mould is a USED tool, and the sim's own wear model already raises the pressure as a tool ages (wearPressureFactor, machine.ts) — so the machine packs above the design point and every part comes out ~0.9 % heavy. Small, physical, and invisible: 100 % of parts stay INSIDE the drawing band while the process centre is off. That is the product's whole claim, and it is now earned rather than staged. --- SEPARATION OF KINDS: partMass_g is a PRODUCT characteristic (drawing, binding). mouldTempC is a RESIN property (norm, binding). The pressures / hot-runner / cushion are MACHINE SETTINGS from the process data sheet (process_estimate, optimisable) and are passed through here untouched. Throwing all three into one file under one 'drawing' stamp is what produced the fiction we just removed.

provenance.$comment: GENERATED — do not hand-edit the part-mass band. Re-run @i3x/sim-schemas scripts/gen-product-spec.ts.

recipe:part_mass_band.drawingStore: windchill.documents (PLM) — content, not just a record
derivation: m_nom = 7.936 cm3 * 1.36 g/cm3 = 10.79296 g -> published 10.79 g   [geometry x density]
LSL   = V(t=1.9 mm) * 1.36 = 10.30923 g -> 10.31 g   [wall tolerance, rounded inward]
USL   = V(t=2.1 mm) * 1.36 = 11.27103 g -> 11.27 g   [wall tolerance, rounded inward]
band midpoint 10.79 g == published nominal 10.79 g (Ca is centred on the nominal)
